name: PlanetScale
description: Managed MySQL-compatible database platform built on Vitess with Git-like branching workflows and non-blocking schema changes

license.comment: PlanetScale is a managed service built on open-source Vitess. The platform itself is proprietary.

hosting-options.comment: Managed service only. PlanetScale Managed allows deployment in customer's own AWS/GCP accounts. Not self-hosted; uses Vitess under the hood which is open source.

initial-release.comment: Company founded 2018. Hosted database platform launched June 2021. GA in November 2021.

primary-language.comment: Built on Vitess which is written in Go. PlanetScale's proprietary components are not disclosed.

data-model.comment: MySQL-compatible relational database. Also offers PostgreSQL support as of 2025.

topology.comment: Each production branch has a single primary MySQL instance and two replicas. Primary handles writes; replicas serve reads.

sharding.comment: Inherits Vitess sharding via Vindexes. Supports hash and range-based sharding. PlanetScale handles resharding operations.

replication-factor.comment: Production branches include 1 primary and 2 replicas across 3 availability zones by default.

multi-region.comment: Supports read-only regions for global distribution. Primary writes in single region; read replicas can be added in any available region.

cap-position.comment: Uses asynchronous replication for multi-region. Read replicas may have replication lag. Prioritizes availability over strict consistency.

consistency-model.comment: Strong consistency on primary reads/writes. Eventual consistency on replica reads due to async replication. Applications can implement read-your-writes patterns.

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID for single-shard transactions via MySQL. Cross-shard transactions supported via Vitess 2PC mode.

transaction-scope.comment: Single-shard transactions are fully ACID. Cross-shard atomic transactions reintroduced in Vitess 21 with improved resilience (experimental).

isolation-levels.comment: MySQL default is REPEATABLE READ. Read-committed available. Cross-shard transactions have limited isolation guarantees.

distributed-transactions.comment: Two-Phase Commit available via Vitess. Atomic distributed transactions reintroduced in Vitess 21 with improved integration.

sql-compatibility.comment: MySQL wire protocol compatible. Standard MySQL drivers work. Some limitations in sharded mode for certain query patterns.

secondary-indexes.comment: Standard MySQL indexes plus Vitess Secondary Vindexes for cross-shard indexing.

joins.comment: VTGate supports distributed JOINs across shards. Query planning optimizes for data locality when possible.

aggregations.comment: Full aggregation support. Vitess splits into local (pushed to MySQL) and global (aggregated at VTGate) operations.

full-text-search.comment: Uses MySQL's built-in full-text search capabilities on underlying MySQL instances.

max-cluster-size.comment: PlanetScale manages hundreds of thousands of database clusters. Individual cluster size depends on plan. Inherits Vitess scalability (YouTube: tens of thousands of nodes).

write-throughput.value: Scales with cluster size (NVMe drives provide unlimited IOPS)
write-throughput.comment: Metal tier uses direct-attached NVMe for maximum IOPS. Throughput scales with compute resources.

read-latency-p99.comment: Metal tier with NVMe drives provides significantly lower latencies than Aurora/Cloud SQL. Exact figures vary by workload.

auto-scaling.comment: Resource-based pricing allows manual scaling. No automatic scale-out. Users upgrade instance size as needed.

hot-spot-handling.comment: PlanetScale/Vitess supports resharding via MoveTables and Reshard workflows. Operations are initiated manually but run with zero downtime.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: PlanetScale handles upgrades as a managed service. Deploy requests enable zero-downtime schema changes.

online-schema-changes.comment: Non-blocking schema changes via deploy requests. Uses shadow table approach (similar to gh-ost). No table locking during migrations.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: PITR available with retention window (default 2 days). Uses WAL replay for Postgres; MySQL backup-based for Vitess.

backup-strategy.comment: Automated daily backups on production branches. Continuous WAL archiving for PITR. On-demand manual backups available.

kubernetes-operator.comment: PlanetScale maintains the official Vitess Operator for Kubernetes. Used for PlanetScale Managed deployments and available for self-hosted Vitess.

rust-driver.comment: Uses MySQL-compatible Rust drivers via MySQL wire protocol. Connection examples provided.

go-driver.comment: Official Go client library for PlanetScale API. Standard MySQL Go drivers work for database connections.

java-driver.comment: Community planetscale-java connector available. Standard MySQL JDBC drivers work via wire protocol.

dotnet-driver.comment: Uses MySQL Connector/NET via MySQL wire protocol compatibility. Connection examples provided.

python-driver.comment: Uses mysqlclient or other MySQL Python drivers via wire protocol. Connection examples for Django provided.

ruby-driver.comment: Uses mysql2 gem via MySQL wire protocol. Rails integration well documented.

c-cpp-driver.comment: Uses MySQL Connector/C via MySQL wire protocol compatibility.

orm-support.comment: Major MySQL-compatible ORMs work. Prisma has official PlanetScale documentation. Rails/ActiveRecord well supported.

open-source.comment: PlanetScale is a proprietary managed service. Built on open-source Vitess (Apache 2.0), but platform code is not open source.

free-tier.comment: Free Hobby tier discontinued in 2024. Paid plans start at $15/month (PS-5) or $39/month (Metal). Postgres non-HA starts at $5/month.

pricing-model.comment: Resource-based pricing. Costs scale with vCPU, memory, storage, and number of nodes. Enterprise plans have custom pricing.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Smallest HA config: PS-5 at $15/month includes 1/16 vCPU per node, 512MB memory, 10GB storage, 3 nodes with 99.99% SLA.

change-data-capture.comment: VStream API for Vitess-based CDC. PlanetScale Connect integrates with Airbyte/Fivetran. Postgres supports logical replication CDC.

watch-subscribe.comment: VStream API allows subscribing to change events. Third-party tools provide webhook integration for real-time CDC streaming.

triggers.comment: MySQL triggers supported on underlying MySQL instances.

event-streaming.comment: Debezium Vitess Connector streams to Kafka. PlanetScale Connect for Airbyte/Fivetran. VStream API for custom integrations.

github-stars.comment: PlanetScale is a managed service without a primary open-source repository. Vitess (underlying technology) has ~21,000 stars.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Limited Stack Overflow presence. Community support primarily via GitHub Discussions and Discord.
